entries.title: Welcome to Python.org
entries.summary:
* Latest Python version: 3.14.5
* Python 3.15.0 beta 1 is available
* Python documentation: docs.python.org
* Python standard library includes various modules and packages
* Web development frameworks: Django, Pyramid, Bottle, Tornado, Flask, Litestar, FastAPI
* GUI development libraries: tkInter, PyGObject, PyQt, PySide, Kivy, wxPython, DearPyGui
* AI and Machine Learning libraries: PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn, Transformers, Anthropic, LangChain
* Scientific and Numeric libraries: SciPy, Pandas, IPython
* System Administration tools: Ansible, Salt, OpenStack, xonsh

entries.title: Online Python - IDE, Editor, Compiler, Interpreter
entries.summary:
* Online Python IDE provides a free, integrated development environment for Python.
* Python is a general-purpose, open-source programming language first released in 1991.
* Python is widely used for web development, mobile apps, data analytics, and machine learning.
* Python has a simple syntax and is easy to read and write.
* Key features of Python include simplicity, versatility, and a large community.
* Python has extensive support libraries, including NumPy and Pandas.
* Third-party modules contribute to Python's power and functionality.
* Online Python IDE allows for immediate execution of Python code in a web browser.
* Python code can be saved and shared online for access from any location.
* Popular resources for learning Python include python.org, Wikipedia, and w3schools.com.
* Online Python Compiler supports Python 3.12 for interactive program execution
* Code editor features include dark and light themes, customizable themes, and undo/redo options
* Expandable output terminal for displaying program results
* Interactive Python Shell with advanced Python module support (Pandas, NumPy)
* Coding sharing functionality allows saving code in the cloud
* Supported keyboard shortcuts: F8/F10/F9 for run/save/share code
* ACE code editor powers the Online Python IDE
* No data is saved on the server, except for shared code with a selected expiry period
* Code can be downloaded locally or saved online for access anywhere
* Online-ide.com supports learning and practicing othe

entries.title: Online Python Compiler (Interpreter) - Programiz
entries.summary:
* Online Python compiler supports Python 3
* Code can be written and run directly in the online editor
* Output is displayed in a separate section
* Code can be shared via a generated link
* Compiler supports basic Python syntax, including print statements
* Online editor provides a "Run" button to execute the code
* Output can be cleared using the "Clear" button
* Compiler is suitable for testing and running small Python programs
* Supports sharing code on social media platforms
* Provides a "Copy Link" feature to copy the code link to the clipboard

entries.title: Python Tutorial - W3Schools
entries.summary:
* Python syntax is used to define the structure of Python code.
* Variables in Python are defined using the `=` operator, e.g., `x = 5`.
* Python data types include Numbers, Strings, Booleans, Lists, Tuples, Sets, and Dictionaries.
* Python operators include Arithmetic, Assignment, Comparison, Logical, Identity, and Membership operators.
* Control structures in Python include If-Else statements, For loops, and While loops.
* Functions in Python are defined using the `def` keyword, e.g., `def greet():`.
* Modules in Python are used to organize and reuse code, imported using the `import` statement.
* Python supports object-oriented programming (OOP) concepts, including Classes and Objects.
* Error handling in Python is done using Try-Except blocks, e.g., `try: ... except: ...`.
* Python has built-in support for regular expressions (RegEx) and JSON data format.
* Python supports object-oriented programming (OOP) concepts like classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation.
* Python has built-in data structures like lists, stacks, queues, linked lists, hash tables, trees, and graphs.
* Python supports file handling operations like reading, writing, and deleting files.
* Python has libraries like NumPy, Pandas, and SciPy for scientific computing and data analysis.
* Python has frameworks like Django for web development and Matplotlib for data visualization.
* Python supports machine learning concepts like linear regression, polynomial regression, and decision trees

entries.title: Python (programming language) - Wikipedia
entries.summary:
* Paradigm: Multi-paradigm, object-oriented, procedural, functional, structured, reflective
* Typing discipline: Duck, dynamic, strong, optional type annotations
* Memory management: Garbage-collected
* OS: Cross-platform
* License: Python Software Foundation License
* Filename extensions: .py, .pyc, .pyd, .pyi, .pyw, .pyz
* Major implementations: CPython, PyPy, MicroPython, CircuitPython, IronPython, Jython
* Influenced by: ABC, Ada, ALGOL 68, APL, C, C++, CLU, Dylan, Haskell, Icon, Lisp
* Influenced: Apache Groovy, Boo, Cobra, CoffeeScript, D, F#, GDScript, Go, JavaScript, Julia 
* Stable release: 3.14.5
* Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language with emphasis on code readability and simplicity.
* It supports multiple programming paradigms, including object-oriented programming and dynamic typing.
* Python has a "batteries-included" standard library and uses significant indentation and "plain English" naming.
* The language features garbage collection and optional static typing (added in Python 3.5).
* Python has a five-year support policy, with versions 3.10-3.14 currently supported.
* The language is widely used in machine learning and is a popular introductory programming language.
* Python's development began in 1989, with the first release (0.9.0) in 1991.
* Guido van Rossum is the creator of Python and was the lead developer until 2018.
* Python 3.0 (released in 2008) was a major revision with some backward-incompatibility.
* The language is ranked
